topic: In China employee termination: decision-tree payroll operating model for foreign employers
primary_keyword: China employee termination decision tree payroll

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Provides framework for termination legality and compensation exposure.
Supports need for evidence-backed decisioning and process integrity.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Anchors obligations relevant to termination-month closure and payroll-linked statutory processing.
Supports complete offboarding controls after final payout decisions.

sources.title: People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
key_points_used: Provides practical tax context for component-level final pay treatment.

sources.title: Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers
key_points_used: Reinforces operational discipline and documentation as major control levers.
Supports decision-structured governance for multinational employers.

consensus: Termination workflows benefit from explicit branch decisions instead of one-size-fits-all processing.
Evidence and approval quality should determine payout scope.
Escalation thresholds reduce ad hoc release risk.
uncertainties: City-level interpretation and evidence thresholds can vary.
Some technical treatment details require local confirmation.
English-language summaries may not capture all Chinese-language implementation detail.
practical_implication_for_foreign_employ: Apply a three-branch decision tree (standard / contested / high-risk) with branch-specific payout and escalation rules.
